description: This context folder provides resources and information for students pursuing Anthropology degrees or minors at Southwestern University. It covers topics like career paths, faculty profiles, course descriptions, and opportunities for internships and graduate school. You'll also find details on honor society membership and relevant professional links.
keywords: what can I do with an anthropology degree
what are the requirements for AKD

description: Covers CS B.A., B.S., Minor, and Computational Mathematics degrees. Topics include programming courses (Java, C++, Haskell), algorithm analysis, artificial intelligence, computer systems, and senior software engineering capstone. Also includes department faculty, ACM/UPE clubs, and course placement via AP or transfer credit.

description: Covers Math B.A., B.S., and Minor degrees. Topics include calculus (I, II, III), linear algebra, real analysis, complex analysis, algebraic structures, and mathematical modeling. Includes ALEKS math placement guidelines, Math Club, Pi Mu Epsilon honor society, and research opportunities like SCOPE.

This context provides information about the Pre-Engineering Pathway at Southwestern University, including required courses and degree options. It details dual degree programs with Washington University in St. Louis and Texas Tech University, as well as a physics-to-engineering graduate pathway. The content also lists specific courses within chemistry, computer science, math, and physics relevant to pre-engineering students.
